DECLARATION OF MARIA SANTOS IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS MOTION FOR DISCHARGE OF STAKEHOLDER AND REQUEST FOR AWARD OF REASONABLE ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS (TRANSACTION ID # 15053164) FILED BY PLAINTIFF CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION FOR DISCHARGE OF STAKEHOLDER AND REQUEST FOR A WARD OF REASONABLE ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS IS GRANTED. THE DISCHARGE IS LIMITED TO THE FUNDS THAT WERE DEPOSITED WITH THE COURT AND DOES NOT IN ANY WAY ADJUDICATE ANY PENDING OR FUTURE CLAIMS AGAINST CHICAGO TITLE FOR ALLEGED BREACH OF CONTRACT OR FAILURE TO PROPERLY HANDLE THE ESCROW OTHER THAN A CLAIM ASSERTING THAT THE FUNDS DEPOSITED WITH THE COURT SHOULD HAVE BEEN PAID TO ONE OR MORE OF THE DEFENDANTS IN THIS ACTION PER THE PARTIES' ESCROW CONTRACT. PLAINTIFF IS AWARDED FEES AND COSTS OF $18,654.75. IGLESIA NI CRISTO'S "DEFENSE" BASED ON FORUM NON CONVENIENS LACKS MERIT BOTH PROCEDURALLY AND SUBSTANTIVELY. THE COURT HAS RECEIVED PLAINTIFF'S PROPOSED ORDER AND IS INCLINED TO SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER AFTER VERIFYING WHETHER THE INDICATED FORM OF PAYMENT IS FEASIBLE FOR THE COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR #5963; DEPT. 302 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Discharge Of Stakeholder And Request For A Ward Of Reasonable Attorneys Fees And Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Apr-15-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MARK TOWNSEND of Hershorin & Henry, LLP, Tel No. (949) 859-5600 appearing telephonically for Plaintiff Chicago Title Company;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY'S Motion For Discharge Of Stakeholder And Request For A Ward Of Reasonable Attorneys Fees And Costs is granted. The discharge is limited to the funds that were deposited with the court and does not in any way adjudicate any pending or future claims against Chicago Title for alleged breach of contract or failure to properly handle the escrow other than a claim asserting that the funds deposited with the court should have been paid to one or more of the defendants in this action per the parties' escrow contract. Plaintiff is awarded fees and costs of $18,654.75. Iglesia Ni Cristo's "defense" based on forum non conveniens lacks merit both procedurally and substantively. THE COURT HAS RECEIVED PLAINTIFF'S PROPOSED ORDER AND IS INCLINED TO SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER AFTER VERIFYING WHETHER THE INDICATED FORM OF PAYMENT IS FEASIBLE FOR THE COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/HK)
